note: Candidate applied EAPI 7->8 correctly but omitted the key functional edits: the added `elisp-enable-tests ert test` line (likely the actual reason for the -r1 revbump), the 9999/git-r3 conditional restructuring of SRC_URI+KEYWORDS, and the DOCS array conversion. HOMEPAGE trailing slash and DOCS form treated leniently but the tests enablement is a real omission. Ebuild not broken.

Candidate only bumped CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION 3->4 (reference set it to 9). It missed all the real edits: added CABAL_CHDEPS block, added >=dev-haskell/text-1.1.1 RDEPEND, loosened unicode-data upper bound <0.5 -> <0.9, ghc 8.4.3 -> 9.0.2, cabal 2.2.0.1 -> 3.4.1.0, quickcheck upper <2.15 -> <2.18 plus dev-haskell/text test dep, removed IUSE="". The revision value itself is also wrong (4 vs 9).

Candidate is the old 1.1.3.0 ebuild with only the version bumped; it kept everything identical (explicitly noting deps couldn't be verified offline). Missed all reference functional edits: CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=3 added; IUSE +gmp -> +simd; RDEPEND ghc 8.4.3 -> 9.0.2 and dropped gmp block; cabal 2.2.0.1 -> 3.4.1.0; test deps rewritten with upper bounds; added src_prepare cabal-mksetup; cabal_flag gmp libgmp -> simd simd.

All functional edits reproduced: EAPI 7->8, HOMEPAGE trailing slash, git-r3/9999 conditional SRC_URI+KEYWORDS block, DOCS string->array. Only cosmetic differences (copyright year 2023 vs 2025, EVAL-NOTE line).

Candidate correctly made the core EAPI 7->8 bump (the primary functional change of the r1 revbump). It omitted the reference's restructuring that adds the 9999/git-r3 live-ebuild conditional (moving SRC_URI+KEYWORDS into the else branch). That scaffold is a no-op for the 0.6-r1 build (else branch equals prior behavior), so the ebuild still builds equivalently, but it is a distinct edit the reference made that the candidate lacks. RDEPEND reformat and HOMEPAGE trailing slash are cosmetic. Partial.

Candidate renamed to 0.1.2 but kept dep block identical to the old version. Missed every functional edit: ghc 8.8.1->9.0.2, cabal 3.0.0.0->3.4.1.0, singleton-bool 0.1.5->0.1.8, tasty 1.4/1.5->1.5/1.6, tasty-expected-failure restructure, and CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION (dropped instead of set to 1). Only cosmetic changes reproduced.

Candidate only removed CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION and bumped the filename version; it kept all the old 1.3.15 dependency bounds. It also wrongly retained the dev-haskell/text || block that the reference removed entirely. Candidate self-flagged it could not verify bounds offline.

Candidate left all dependency bounds at 0.1 values and only dropped CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION. Missed ghc 8.8.1->9.0.2, cabal 3.0.0.0->3.4.1.0, quickcheck upper 2.15->2.18, quickcheck-instances 0.4->0.5, test-framework(-quickcheck2)->tasty/tasty-quickcheck, revision 2->1 (reference keeps it at 1, candidate wrongly deleted it), and did not drop pkg_pretend.

Candidate is byte-identical to the before ebuild (plus an EVAL-NOTE admitting it made no change). Version bumped correctly, but candidate kept the OLD dep bounds instead of the reference's updated ones: missing ghc>=9.0.2, uuid-types 1.0.6, random 1.2.1.2, network-info 0.2/0.3, cabal 3.4.1.0, tasty/quickcheck/tasty-hunit upper-bound bumps, and removal of the text || block. Extra/wrong: dropped CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION which the reference retains (=2).

Candidate is a near-verbatim copy of the before ebuild plus an EVAL-NOTE; reproduced none of the reference's functional edits (EAPI 7->8, git-r3 9999 conditional/HOMEPAGE change, DOCS string->array, elisp-enable-tests ert test).

Reference revbump = EAPI 7->8 plus adding the 9999/git-r3 live-ebuild branch (SRC_URI kept as ${P}.tar.gz, KEYWORDS moved inside else-branch, DOCS as array, HOMEPAGE trailing slash). Candidate kept EAPI=7, did none of these, and instead invented a distfile rename to ${P}.gh.tar.gz that the reference never made. All real edits missed; introduced a wrong one.

Candidate only bumped the filename; kept all old dependency bounds. Candidate kept all the old 0.1 dependency bounds and structure instead of the reference's 0.1.3 updates: ghc 8.8.1 (ref 9.0.2), cabal 3.0.0.0 (ref 3.4.1.0), quickcheck <2.15 (ref <2.18), quickcheck-instances <0.4 (ref <0.5), retained test-framework/test-framework-quickcheck2 (ref replaced with tasty/tasty-quickcheck), kept pkg_pretend block (ref removed it). Candidate is the unchanged 1.1.3.0 ebuild. Missed all functional edits: IUSE +gmp->+simd, ghc >=8.4.3->9.0.2, removed gmp? dep, cabal 2.2.0.1->3.4.1.0, rewritten bounded test deps, added src_prepare+cabal-mksetup, cabal_flag gmp libgmp->simd simd, CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION=3. Candidate correctly did EAPI 7->8. But it omitted the reference's main restructure: wrapping SRC_URI/S/KEYWORDS in a PV==*9999* branch with git-r3 inherit + EGIT_REPO_URI for a live ebuild. Cosmetic-only misses (HOMEPAGE trailing slash, DOCS string vs array) ignored. The git-r3 live-branch addition is a real functional edit that is missing.

Candidate left the ebuild effectively identical to 1.4.27 (only the filename bumped). It omitted every real 1.4.33 change: new MY_URI upload path (2025/03), linux-* SRC_URI filenames, and the entire src_install restructure (myarch dir instead of release/build, dolib.so ${PN}.so instead of .so.${PV} with swapped dosym targets, doins ftd2xx.h/WinTypes.h from cwd, dodoc examples, release-notes.txt instead of release/ReadMe.txt). Its own EVAL-NOTE admits it couldn't verify the URL. The result would fail to fetch and fail to install; broken bump.

Candidate is byte-identical to the before ebuild (only an EVAL-NOTE comment added); it reproduced none of the reference's revbump edits: EAPI 7->8, added 9999/git-r3 conditional block, HOMEPAGE trailing slash, DOCS string->array. No functional change made.

Candidate is byte-for-byte the before ebuild (renamed) plus an EVAL-NOTE; it performed none of the reference's dependency changes: ghc 8.8.1->9.0.2, cabal 3.0.0.0->3.4.1.0, removal of foldable1-classes-compat/hashable from RDEPEND, and adding them as test? deps. Candidate correctly dropped both LIBSUBDIR settings (the export and the emake arg), but omitted the reference's replacement line `export LIBDIR="\$(PREFIX)/$(get_libdir)"` in src_configure. Without it the libdir override intended by the change is lost. Copyright-year difference is cosmetic.

Reference -r1 bumped CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION 2->6, ghc dep >=8.8.1 -> >=9.0.2, cabal 3.0.0.0 -> 3.4.1.0, and rewrote tasty/tasty-hunit/tasty-quickcheck bounds. Candidate only touched CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION and set it to the wrong value (3, not 6) and made none of the dependency changes.

Candidate enabled py3.15 (added python3_15) matching the intent, but reference set PYTHON_COMPAT to {12..15}, dropping python3_11. Candidate used {11..15}, keeping python3_11 that the reference removed. Core edit present, one range edit missed.
Candidate enabled py3.14 via PYTHON_COMPAT {11..14}, matching reference. Only difference is cosmetic copyright year (candidate kept 2024 vs reference 2026).
Candidate enabled py3.14 via PYTHON_COMPAT=( python3_{11..14} ), matching the reference. Only difference is copyright year (cosmetic).
Candidate added py3.15 (main intent) but set PYTHON_COMPAT=(python3_{11..15}) whereas reference bumped the lower bound to python3_{12..15}, dropping py3.11. Upper end correct; lower bound not raised.

EAPI 7->8 bump present (correct). Missing the reference's 9999 live-ebuild conditional block (git-r3 inherit + EGIT_REPO_URI, with SRC_URI/KEYWORDS moved into the else branch). HOMEPAGE trailing-slash difference is cosmetic. Ebuild is otherwise valid.

Candidate correctly dropped LIBSUBDIR from src_configure and src_install, but omitted the reference's replacement `export LIBDIR="\$(PREFIX)/$(get_libdir)"` in src_configure, which sets the lib install path. Functional edit missing.

Candidate added py3.15 (PYTHON_COMPAT python3_{11..15}), achieving the stated intent. But reference used python3_{12..15}, also dropping py3.11; candidate kept 3.11, so it omitted the lower-bound bump. Copyright year diff is cosmetic.

Candidate added py3.15 (PYTHON_COMPAT {11..15}) satisfying the enable-3.15 intent, but reference used {12..15}, dropping py3_11. Candidate omitted the py3_11 removal, keeping stale support. Copyright year diff is cosmetic.

Candidate got the structural removals right (dropped CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION, CABAL_CHDEPS block, and the fgl || (...) alternative block), matching the reference. No extra unjustified edits.

Candidate enabled py3.15 (added python3_15) matching the core intent, but missed two reference edits: trimming PYTHON_COMPAT to python3_{12..15} (dropping python3_11 and pypy3_11) and renaming the upstream repo iterative->tqdm in PYPI_VERIFY_REPO and HOMEPAGE. Ebuild still valid.

Candidate bumped CABAL_HACKAGE_REVISION but to 3 (ref went 2->6, wrong value). Missed ghc dep bump (8.8.1->9.0.2), cabal bump (3.0.0.0->3.4.1.0), and the tasty/tasty-hunit/tasty-quickcheck rework to ranged deps. Only the revision-bump intent was partially attempted, and with wrong number.

Candidate enabled py3.14 (core intent met) but kept python3_10 (reference raised floor to 11) and kept bare pypy3 (reference removed it). PYTHON_COMPAT differs: candidate {10..14} pypy3 pypy3_11 vs reference {11..14} pypy3_11.

Reference change is sys-libs/zlib:= -> virtual/zlib:=; candidate made exactly this edit. Only remaining diff is virtual/opengl vs virtual/zlib ordering within COMMON_DEPEND, which is cosmetic.
